title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Speed Booster Test

questions.question: The bases of a hemisphere and a cone are equal. If their height is also the same, then what is the ratio between their curved surface areas?
questions.explanation: Given: Radius of hemisphere (r) = Radius of cone (r). Height of hemisphere = Height of cone (h). Formula Used: Height of hemisphere = r Slant height of cone (l) = r2+h2 \sqrt{r^2 + h^2}r2+h2тАЛтАЛтАЛ Curved Surface Area of hemisphere = 2╧Аr22╧Аr^22╧Аr2тАЛтАЛ Curved Surface Area of cone = ╧Аrl Solution: Since heights are equal, the height of the cone (h) = radius (r). Slant height of the cone (l) = r2+r2=2r2=2r \sqrt{r^2 + r^2} = \sqrt{2r^2} = \sqrt{2}rr2+r2тАЛ=2r2тАЛ=2тАЛrтАЛтАЛ Curved Surface Area of the hemisphere = 2╧Аr22╧Аr^22╧Аr2тАЛтАЛ Curved Surface Area of the cone = ╧Аr(2r)=2╧Аr2╧Аr(\sqrt{2}r) = \sqrt{2}╧Аr^2╧Аr(2тАЛr)=2тАЛ╧Аr2тАЛтАЛ Ratio = 2╧Аr22╧Аr2\frac{2╧Аr^2}{\sqrt{2}╧Аr^2}2тАЛ╧Аr22╧Аr2тАЛтАЛтАЛ = 22=21=2:1 \frac{2}{\sqrt{2}} = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{1} = \sqrt{2} : 12тАЛ2тАЛ=12тАЛтАЛ=2тАЛ:1тАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (d) Exam Hall Method:

questions.question: During a sale, 55% of the goods are sold at a 41% profit, 20% of the remaining goods are sold at a 19% profit and the rest are sold at a 21% loss. If there is an overall profit of x%, then what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Given 55% goods sold at 41% profit 20% of remaining goods sold at 19% profit Rest sold at 21% loss Formula Used Net Profit/Loss % = Weighted average of individual profit/loss percentages. Solution Let total goods be 100. Part 1: 55 goods sold at +41% Remaining goods = 100 - 55 = 45 Part 2: 20% of 45 = 9 goods sold at +19% Part 3: Rest = 45 - 9 = 36 goods sold at -21% Total net profit = 55├Ч41+9├Ч19+36├Ч(тИТ21)55 \times 41 + 9 \times 19 + 36 \times (-21)55├Ч41+9├Ч19+36├Ч(тИТ21)тАЛтАЛ Total net profit = 2255 + 171 - 756 = 1670 Overall percentage = 1670100=16.7%\frac{1670}{100} = 16.7\%1001670тАЛ=16.7%тАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (d) Exam Hall Approach:

questions.question: From the top of a tower, the angles of depression of two obwects, on the ground on the same side of it are observed to be 60┬░ and 30┬░, respectively, and the height of the tower is 450 m. The distance between the obwects (in m) is:
questions.explanation: Given Height of tower (h) = 450 m Angles of depression = 60тИШand30тИШ60^{\circ} and 30^{\circ}60тИШand30тИШтАЛтАЛ Formula Used тАЛtanтБб(╬╕)=PerpendicularBase\tan(\theta) = \frac{\text{Perpendicular}}{\text{Base}}tan(╬╕)=BasePerpendicularтАЛтАЛтАЛ Solution Let the distances of the obwects from the base of the tower be x and y (where y > x). тАЛFor angle 60тИШ:tanтБб(60тИШ)=450x 3=450x\text{For angle}\, 60^{\circ}: \tan(60^{\circ}) = \frac{450}{x} \\\ \\\sqrt{3} = \frac{450}{x}For angle60тИШ:tan(60тИШ)=x450тАЛ 3тАЛ=x450тАЛтАЛтАЛ тАЛx=4503=1503mx = \frac{450}{\sqrt{3}} = 150\sqrt{3} mx=3тАЛ450тАЛ=1503тАЛmтАЛтАЛ For angle 30тИШ:tanтБб(30тИШ)=450y30^{\circ}: \tan(30^{\circ}) = \frac{450}{y}30тИШ:tan(30тИШ)=y450тАЛтАЛтАЛ тАЛ13=450y y=4503m\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} = \frac{450}{y} \\\ \\y = 450\sqrt{3} m3тАЛ1тАЛ=y450тАЛ y=4503тАЛmтАЛтАЛ Distance between obwects = y - x Distance = 4503тИТ1503=3003m450\sqrt{3} - 150\sqrt{3} = 300\sqrt{3} m4503тАЛтИТ1503тАЛ=3003тАЛmтАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: If cot A = 34\frac 3443тАЛтАЛ, and A is an acute angle, then find the value of sin2A - cos3A.
questions.explanation: Given cot A = 34\frac{3}{4}43тАЛтАЛтАЛ A is an acute angle Formula Used: sin 2A = 2sin A cos A cos 3A = 4cos3AтИТ3cosA4cos^3 A - 3cos A4cos3AтИТ3cosAтАЛтАЛ Solution Since cot A =34 \frac{3}{4}43тАЛтАЛ, Base = 3, Perpendicular = 4 Hypotenuse = 5 sin A = 45\frac{4}{5}54тАЛтАЛтАЛ cos A =35 \frac{3}{5}53тАЛтАЛтАЛ sin 2A = 2 ├Ч 45├Ч35=2425=120125\frac{4}{5} ├Ч \frac{3}{5} = \frac{24}{25} = \frac{120}{125}54тАЛ├Ч53тАЛ=2524тАЛ=125120тАЛтАЛтАЛ cos 3A = 4(35)3тИТ3(35)4(\frac{3}{5})^3 - 3(\frac{3}{5})4(53тАЛ)3тИТ3(53тАЛ)тАЛтАЛ cos 3A = 4(27125)тИТ954(\frac{27}{125}) - \frac{9}{5}4(12527тАЛ)тИТ59тАЛтАЛтАЛ cos 3A = 108125тИТ225125=тИТ117125\frac{108}{125} - \frac{225}{125} = -\frac{117}{125}125108тАЛтИТ125225тАЛ=тИТ125117тАЛтАЛтАЛ sin 2A - cos 3A = 120125тИТ(тИТ117125)=120+117125=237125\frac{120}{125} - (-\frac{117}{125})= \frac{120 + 117}{125} = \frac{237}{125} \\\\125120тАЛтИТ(тИТ125117тАЛ)=125120+117тАЛ=125237тАЛтАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: A mixture contains 15% of substance A and 85% of substance B. if you add more of aa so that the new mixture contains 35% A, by what percentage did A increases?
questions.explanation: Given: Initial substance A = 15% Initial substance B = 85% New substance A = 35% Formula Used: Concentration = (PartTotal)├Ч100 (\frac{Part }{ Total}) \times 100(TotalPartтАЛ)├Ч100тАЛтАЛ Solution: Let initial mixture be 100 units. Then A = 15 units, B = 85 units. Let x units of A be added. New mixture total = 100 + x New A = 15 + x тАЛ15+x100+x=35100 15+x100+x=720\frac{15 + x}{100 + x} = \frac{35}{100}\\ \ \\\frac{15 + x}{100 + x} = \frac{7}{20}100+x15+xтАЛ=10035тАЛ 100+x15+xтАЛ=207тАЛтАЛтАЛ 300 + 20x = 700 + 7x 13x = 400 x = 40013тЙИ30.769\frac{400}{13} \approx 30.76913400тАЛтЙИ30.769тАЛтАЛ Percentage increase in A = =x15├Ч100 =4001315├Ч100 =8039├Ч100тЙИ205.13%= \frac{x}{15} \times 100 \\ \ \\= \frac{\frac{400}{13}}{15} \times 100 \\ \ \\= \frac{80}{39} \times 100 \approx 205.13\%=15xтАЛ├Ч100 =1513400тАЛтАЛ├Ч100 =3980тАЛ├Ч100тЙИ205.13%тАЛтАЛтАЛ So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: What is the maximum value that must be assigned to A so that the 8-digit number 713A8845 is divisible by 3?
questions.explanation: Given Number = 713A8845 Condition = Divisible by 3 Formula Used A number is divisible by 3 if the sum of its digits is divisible by 3. Solution Sum of digits = 7 + 1 + 3 + A + 8 + 8 + 4 + 5 Sum = 36 + A For 36 + A to be divisible by 3, A can take values 0, 3, 6, 9. The maximum possible value for a single digit A is 9. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Tewasvi, Trishana and Meena are three sisters. Tewasvi and Trishana are twins. The sum of the ages of Tewasvi and Trishana is the same as that of Meena. Four years earlier, the ratio of the ages of Tewasvi and Meena was 1:3. What will be the age of Trishana 4 years from now?
questions.explanation: Given Tewasvi (T) and Trishana (Tr) are twins T = Tr T + Tr = Meena (M) Ratio 4 years earlier = 1 : 3 Solution Since T = Tr, T + T = M 2T = M Four years ago: тАЛTтИТ4MтИТ4=13\frac{T - 4}{M - 4} = \frac{1}{3}MтИТ4TтИТ4тАЛ=31тАЛтАЛтАЛ 3(T - 4) = M - 4 3T - 12 = 2T - 4 T = 8 Trishana's present age = 8 years Trishana's age 4 years from now = 8 + 4 = 12 years Final Answer So the correct answer is (c) тАЛ Exam Hall Method:

questions.question: The average of 150 numbers is 75. It is found that while calculating the average, two numbers 71 and 16 were wrongly read as 17 and 61. What is the average if the readings of these two numbers are recorded correctly now?
questions.explanation: Given Total numbers = 150 Incorrect average = 75 Correct numbers = 71 and 16 Wrong numbers = 17 and 61 Formula Used Correct Sum = Incorrect Sum - Wrong Values + Correct Values Correct Average = Correct SumTotal numbers \frac{\text{Correct Sum}}{\text{Total numbers}}Total numbersCorrect SumтАЛтАЛтАЛ Solution Incorrect Sum = 150├Ч75=11250150 \times 75 = 11250150├Ч75=11250тАЛтАЛ Correct Sum = 11250 - 17 - 61 + 71 + 16 Correct Sum = 11250 - 78 + 87 Correct Sum = 11250 + 9 = 11259 Correct Average = 11259150\frac{11259}{150}15011259тАЛтАЛтАЛ Correct Average = 75.06 Final Answer So the correct answer is (c) Exam Hall Method: тАЛ

questions.question: A sum of money triples itself at a certain rate of compound interest in 3 years. In how many years will it amount to 27 times of itself?
questions.explanation: Given: A sum triples itself in 3 years at compound interest. Formula Used: If a sum becomes xxx times in ttt years at compound interest, it will become xnx^nxn times in n├Чtn \times tn├Чt years. Solution: The sum becomes 313^131 times in 3 years. We need to find the time it takes to become 27 times. We can write 27 as 333^333. Here, n=3n = 3n=3. Time taken to become 333^333 times = 3├Ч3=93 \times 3 = 93├Ч3=9 years. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: If sinx = cos(3x-20┬░), what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Given: тАЛsinтБбx=cosтБб(3xтИТ20тИШ)\sin x = \cos(3x - 20^{\circ})sinx=cos(3xтИТ20тИШ)тАЛтАЛ Formula Used: тАЛsinтБб╬╕=cosтБб(90тИШтИТ╬╕)\sin \theta = \cos(90^{\circ} - \theta)sin╬╕=cos(90тИШтИТ╬╕)тАЛтАЛ Solution: Using the complementary angle identity: тАЛcosтБб(90тИШтИТx)=cosтБб(3xтИТ20тИШ)\cos(90^{\circ} - x) = \cos(3x - 20^{\circ})cos(90тИШтИТx)=cos(3xтИТ20тИШ)тАЛтАЛ Equating the angles: тАЛ90тИШтИТx=3xтИТ20тИШ90^{\circ} - x = 3x - 20^{\circ}90тИШтИТx=3xтИТ20тИШтАЛтАЛ тАЛ90тИШ+20тИШ=3x+x90^{\circ} + 20^{\circ} = 3x + x90тИШ+20тИШ=3x+xтАЛтАЛ тАЛ110тИШ=4x110^{\circ} = 4x110тИШ=4xтАЛтАЛ x = 110тИШ4=27.5тИШ \frac{110^{\circ}}{4} = 27.5^{\circ}4110тИШтАЛ=27.5тИШтАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: If a + 1/a = 4, then 4a2+46a2+10a+6\frac{4a^2+4}{6a^2+10a+6}6a2+10a+64a2+4тАЛтАЛ=?
questions.explanation: Given: a + 1a\frac{1}{a}a1тАЛтАЛ = 4 Solution: Expression is: = 4a2+46a2+10a+6\frac{4a^2 + 4}{6a^2 + 10a + 6}6a2+10a+64a2+4тАЛтАЛтАЛ Divide the numerator and the denominator by a = 4a+4a6a+10+6a\frac{4a + \frac{4}{a}}{6a + 10 + \frac{6}{a}}6a+10+a6тАЛ4a+a4тАЛтАЛтАЛтАЛ Take out common factors = 4(a+1a)6(a+1a)+10\frac{4(a + \frac{1}{a})}{6(a + \frac{1}{a}) + 10}6(a+a1тАЛ)+104(a+a1тАЛ)тАЛтАЛтАЛ Substitute the given value = 4├Ч46├Ч4+10\frac{4 ├Ч 4}{6 ├Ч 4 + 10}6├Ч4+104├Ч4тАЛтАЛтАЛ = 1624+10\frac{16}{24 + 10}24+1016тАЛтАЛтАЛ = 1634 \frac{16}{34}3416тАЛтАЛтАЛ = 817\frac{8}{17}178тАЛтАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Evaluate: (8.85├Ч78.3225+1.15├Ч1.3225)(79.645тИТ10.1775)=?\frac{(8.85├Ч78.3225+1.15├Ч1.3225)}{(79.645-10.1775)}=?(79.645тИТ10.1775)(8.85├Ч78.3225+1.15├Ч1.3225)тАЛ=?тАЛтАЛ
questions.explanation: Given Expression = 8.85├Ч78.3225+1.15├Ч1.322579.645тИТ10.1775\frac{8.85 \times 78.3225 + 1.15 \times 1.3225}{79.645 - 10.1775}79.645тИТ10.17758.85├Ч78.3225+1.15├Ч1.3225тАЛтАЛтАЛ Formula Used тАЛa3+b3=(a+b)(a2тИТab+b2)a^{3} + b^{3} = (a + b)(a^{2} - ab + b^{2})a3+b3=(a+b)(a2тИТab+b2)тАЛтАЛ Solution Let a = 8.85 and b = 1.15 Notice that 78.3225 = (8.85)2=a2(8.85)^{2} = a^{2}(8.85)2=a2тАЛтАЛ Notice that 1.3225 = (1.15)2=b2(1.15)^{2} = b^{2}(1.15)2=b2тАЛтАЛ The numerator becomes: тАЛa├Чa2+b├Чb2=a3+b3a \times a^{2} + b \times b^{2} = a^{3} + b^{3}a├Чa2+b├Чb2=a3+b3тАЛтАЛ In the denominator: ab=8.85├Ч1.15=10.1775 a2+b2=78.3225+1.3225=79.645ab = 8.85 \times 1.15 = 10.1775 \\\ \\a^{2} + b^{2} = 78.3225 + 1.3225 = 79.645ab=8.85├Ч1.15=10.1775 a2+b2=78.3225+1.3225=79.645тАЛ So the denominator is a2+b2тИТaba^{2} + b^{2} - aba2+b2тИТabтАЛтАЛ The entire expression reduces to: тАЛa3+b3a2тИТab+b2\frac{a^{3} + b^{3}}{a^{2} - ab + b^{2}}a2тИТab+b2a3+b3тАЛтАЛ This simplifies to a + b Result = 8.85 + 1.15 = 10 Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: If тИаP, тИаQ, тИаR and тИаS of a quadrilateral PQRS, taken in order, are in the ratio 4:3:7:6, then quadrilateral PQRS will be of which type?
questions.explanation: Given Ratio of angles P : Q : R : S = 4 : 3 : 7 : 6 Formula Used Sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral = 360 degrees If consecutive interior angles are supplementary, lines are parallel. Solution Sum of the ratio parts = 4 + 3 + 7 + 6 = 20 Value of 1 part = 36020=18тИШ \frac{360}{20} = 18^\circ20360тАЛ=18тИШтАЛтАЛ Angle P = 4├Ч18=72тИШ4 \times 18 = 72^\circ4├Ч18=72тИШтАЛтАЛ Angle Q = 3├Ч18=54тИШ 3 \times 18 = 54^\circ3├Ч18=54тИШтАЛтАЛ Angle R = 7├Ч18=126тИШ 7 \times 18 = 126^\circ7├Ч18=126тИШтАЛтАЛ Angle S = 6├Ч18=108тИШ6 \times 18 = 108^\circ6├Ч18=108тИШтАЛтАЛ Checking sum of adwacent angles: Angle P + Angle S =72тИШ+108тИШ=180тИШ 72^\circ + 108^\circ = 180^\circ72тИШ+108тИШ=180тИШтАЛтАЛ Angle Q + Angle R = 54тИШ+126тИШ=180тИШ54^\circ + 126^\circ = 180^\circ54тИШ+126тИШ=180тИШтАЛтАЛ Since adwacent angles on the sides PS and QR are supplementary, the side PQ is parallel to the side RS. Therefore, exactly one pair of opposite sides is parallel, making it a trapezium. Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: P can finish a certain work in the same time in which Q and R together can finish it. If P and Q together could finish the work in 20 days and R alone in 100 days, Q alone can finish the work in:
questions.explanation: Given: Time of P = Time of (Q + R) Time of (P + Q) = 20 days Time of R = 100 days Formula Used: Work done per day = 1Total Days \frac{1}{\text{Total Days}}Total Days1тАЛтАЛтАЛ Solution: From the given time equality, their rates of work relate as: тАЛ1P=1Q+1R\frac{1}{P} = \frac{1}{Q} + \frac{1}{R}P1тАЛ=Q1тАЛ+R1тАЛтАЛтАЛ The combined rate of P and Q is: тАЛ1P+1Q=120\frac{1}{P} + \frac{1}{Q} = \frac{1}{20}P1тАЛ+Q1тАЛ=201тАЛтАЛтАЛ The rate of R is: тАЛ1R=1100\frac{1}{R} = \frac{1}{100}R1тАЛ=1001тАЛтАЛтАЛ Substitute 1P\frac{1}{P}P1тАЛтАЛ into the combined rate equation: тАЛ(1Q+1R)+1Q=120(\frac{1}{Q} + \frac{1}{R}) + \frac{1}{Q} = \frac{1}{20}(Q1тАЛ+R1тАЛ)+Q1тАЛ=201тАЛтАЛтАЛ тАЛ2Q+1100=120\frac{2}{Q} + \frac{1}{100} = \frac{1}{20}Q2тАЛ+1001тАЛ=201тАЛтАЛтАЛ тАЛ2Q=120тИТ1100 2Q=5100тИТ1100=4100 2Q=125 Q=50 days\frac{2}{Q} = \frac{1}{20} - \frac{1}{100} \\ \ \\\frac{2}{Q} = \frac{5}{100} - \frac{1}{100} = \frac{4}{100}\\ \ \\\frac{2}{Q} = \frac{1}{25}\\ \ \\Q = 50 \text{ days}Q2тАЛ=201тАЛтИТ1001тАЛ Q2тАЛ=1005тАЛтИТ1001тАЛ=1004тАЛ Q2тАЛ=251тАЛ Q=50 daysтАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (c) Exam Hall Method:

questions.question: The simple interest for a sum of Rs. 6200 for 4 years is Rs. 80 more than the simple interest for Rs. 5800 for the same duration, the interest rate being same in both cases. What is the rate of interest?
questions.explanation: Given Principal 1 (P1) = Rs. 6200 Principal 2 (P2) = Rs. 5800 Time (T) = 4 years Difference in Simple Interest = Rs. 80 Formula Used тАЛSI=P├ЧR├ЧT100SI = \frac{P \times R \times T}{100}SI=100P├ЧR├ЧTтАЛтАЛтАЛ Difference in SI = (P1тИТP2)├ЧR├ЧT100\frac{(P1 - P2) \times R \times T}{100}100(P1тИТP2)├ЧR├ЧTтАЛтАЛтАЛ Solution Difference in Principals = 6200 - 5800 = 400 The difference in Simple Interest is due to this difference in Principal over 4 years. тАЛ80=400├ЧR├Ч4100 80=16R R=8016 R=5%80 = \frac{400 \times R \times 4}{100} \\\ \\80 = 16R\\\ \\R = \frac{80}{16}\\\ \\R = 5 \%80=100400├ЧR├Ч4тАЛ 80=16R R=1680тАЛ R=5%тАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: Within a circle, two parallel chords are positioned such that the center lies between them if the distance between these chords is 14 cm , and the length of one chord is 12 cm having radius of 10 cm , what is the length of the other chord?
questions.explanation: Given: Radius of the circle, r = 10 cm Length of first chord, AB = 12 cm Distance between the two parallel chords = 14 cm Formula Used: Perpendicular distance from center to a chord bisects the chord. d = r2тИТ(chord2)2 \sqrt{r^2 - (\frac{\text{chord}}{2})^2}r2тИТ(2chordтАЛ)2тАЛтАЛтАЛ Solution: Let the distance from the center to the first chord be d1.d_1.d1тАЛ.тАЛтАЛ тАЛd1=102тИТ(122)2d_1 = \sqrt{10^2 - (\frac{12}{2})^2}d1тАЛ=102тИТ(212тАЛ)2тАЛтАЛтАЛ d1=100тИТ36=64d_1 = \sqrt{100 - 36} = \sqrt{64}d1тАЛ=100тИТ36тАЛ=64тАЛтАЛ = 8 cm The total distance between the chords is 14 cm, and the center lies between them. Let the distance from the center to the second chord be d2.d_2.d2тАЛ.тАЛтАЛ тАЛd1+d2=14d_1 + d_2 = 14d1тАЛ+d2тАЛ=14тАЛтАЛ тАЛ8+d2=148 + d_2 = 148+d2тАЛ=14тАЛтАЛ d2d_2d2тАЛтАЛ = 6 cm Now, calculate the half-length of the second chord: тАЛCD2=r2тИТd22\frac{\text{CD}}{2} = \sqrt{r^2 - d_2^2}2CDтАЛ=r2тИТd22тАЛтАЛтАЛтАЛ тАЛCD2=102тИТ62\frac{\text{CD}}{2} = \sqrt{10^2 - 6^2}2CDтАЛ=102тИТ62тАЛтАЛ CD2=100тИТ36=64\frac{\text{CD}}{2} = \sqrt{100 - 36} = \sqrt{64}2CDтАЛ=100тИТ36тАЛ=64тАЛтАЛ = 8 cm The total length of the second chord is: CD = 2 ├Ч 8 = 16 cm Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A and B start a business by investing тВ╣29,000 and тВ╣95,000, respectively. B withdraws his investment after 6 months, and then C woins the business with an investment of тВ╣67,000 for the remaining period of the year. If 15% of the total profit is тВ╣6,600, what is BтАЩs share of the profit?
questions.explanation: Given A's investment = тВ╣29,000 for 12 months B's investment = тВ╣95,000 for 6 months C's investment = тВ╣67,000 for 6 months 15% of Total Profit = тВ╣6,600 Formula Used Profit Ratio = Ratio of (Investment ├Ч\times ├ЧтАЛTime) Solution Total Profit = 6,6000.15=44,000 \frac{6,600}{0.15} = 44,0000.156,600тАЛ=44,000тАЛтАЛ Ratio of profits A : B : C = (29000├Ч12):(95000├Ч6):(67000├Ч6)(29000 \times 12) : (95000 \times 6) : (67000 \times 6)(29000├Ч12):(95000├Ч6):(67000├Ч6)тАЛтАЛ Dividing by 6000: A : B : C = (29├Ч2):(95├Ч1):(67├Ч1)=58:95:67 (29 \times 2) : (95 \times 1) : (67 \times 1) = 58 : 95 : 67(29├Ч2):(95├Ч1):(67├Ч1)=58:95:67тАЛтАЛ Total parts = 58 + 95 + 67 = 220 B's share =95220├Ч44000= \frac{95}{220} \times 44000=22095тАЛ├Ч44000тАЛтАЛ B's share = 95├Ч200=19,00095 \times 200 = 19,00095├Ч200=19,000тАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: If the income of Raghu is 40% more than Kumar, then the income of Kumar is less than that of Raghu by what percent?
questions.explanation: Given Raghu's income is 40% more than Kumar's income Formula Used Percentage Less = DifferenceReference Value├Ч100 \frac{\text{Difference}}{\text{Reference Value}} \times 100Reference ValueDifferenceтАЛ├Ч100тАЛтАЛ Solution Let the income of Kumar be 100. Income of Raghu = 100+40% of 100=140100 + 40\% \text{ of } 100 = 140100+40% of 100=140тАЛтАЛ Difference between their incomes = 140 - 100 = 40 We need to find how much less Kumar's income is compared to Raghu's. Percentage Less = 40140├Ч100\frac{40}{140} \times 10014040тАЛ├Ч100%тАЛ Percentage Less = 27├Ч100%\frac{2}{7} \times 100 \%72тАЛ├Ч100%тАЛ Percentage Less = 2007% \frac{200}{7} \%7200тАЛ%тАЛтАЛ Percentage Less = 2847%28 \frac{4}{7} \%2874тАЛ%тАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (c) Exam Hall Method:

questions.question: A cistern has two inlets I1 and I2 which can fill it in 16 hours and 20 hours, respectively. An outlet can empty the full cistern in 12 hours. If all the three pipes are opened together in the empty cistern, how much time will they take to fill the cistern completely?
questions.explanation: Given Time taken by I1 = 16 hours Time taken by I2 = 20 hours Time taken by outlet = 12 hours Formula Used Total Work = LCM of times Efficiency = Total WorkTime\frac{\text{Total Work}}{\text{Time}}TimeTotal WorkтАЛтАЛтАЛ Solution Total capacity = LCM(16, 20, 12) = 240 units Efficiency of I1 = 24016\frac{240}{16}16240тАЛтАЛ = +15 units/hour Efficiency of I2 = 24020\frac{240}{20} 20240тАЛтАЛ= +12 units/hour Efficiency of outlet = 240тИТ12 \frac{240}{-12}тИТ12240тАЛтАЛ = -20 units/hour Net efficiency = 15 + 12 - 20 = 7 units/hour Time taken to fill =2407=3427 \frac{240}{7} = 34 \frac{2}{7}7240тАЛ=3472тАЛтАЛ hours Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Arrange the following values in ascending order: тАЛ174,413,412,194\frac{17}{4}, 4 \frac{1}{3}, 4 \frac{1}{2}, \frac{19}{4}417тАЛ,431тАЛ,421тАЛ,419тАЛтАЛтАЛ
questions.explanation: Given Fractions to be ordered: 174,413,412,and194\frac{17}{4}, 4\frac{1}{3}, 4\frac{1}{2}, and \frac{19}{4}417тАЛ,431тАЛ,421тАЛ,and419тАЛтАЛтАЛ Solution Convert the given values to decimal form: тАЛ174=4.25 413=133тЙИ4.333 412=4.5 194=4.75\frac{17}{4} = 4.25 \\\ \\4\frac{1}{3} = \frac{13}{3} \approx 4.333\\\ \\4\frac{1}{2} = 4.5\\\ \\\frac{19}{4} = 4.75417тАЛ=4.25 431тАЛ=313тАЛтЙИ4.333 421тАЛ=4.5 419тАЛ=4.75тАЛтАЛ Arranging them in ascending order (smallest to largest): 4.25 < 4.333 < 4.5 < 4.75 This corresponds directly to: тАЛ174<413<412<194\frac{17}{4} < 4\frac{1}{3} < 4\frac{1}{2} < \frac{19}{4}417тАЛ<431тАЛ<421тАЛ<419тАЛтАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Kiran buys an article at 12% below its cost and sells it at 12% above its cost. Find his profit percentage.
questions.explanation: Given: Buying price = 12% below original cost. Selling price = 12% above original cost. Formula Used: Profit = Selling Price (SP) - Actual Cost Price (CP) Profit % = ProfitActual CP\frac{Profit}{Actual\ CP}Actual CPProfitтАЛтАЛ ├Ч 100 Solution: Let the original cost of the article be 100 units. Actual Cost Price (CP) for Kiran = 100 - 12% of 100 = 88 units. Selling Price (SP) = 100 + 12% of 100 = 112 units. Profit = SP - Actual CP = 112 - 88 = 24 units. Profit % =2488 \frac{24}{88}8824тАЛтАЛ ├Ч 100 Profit % = 311├Ч100=30011 \frac{3}{11} ├Ч 100 = \frac{300}{11}113тАЛ├Ч100=11300тАЛтАЛтАЛ Profit % = 27.27% Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: A closed cylindrical tank with a height of 1 m and a base diameter of 140 cm must be constructed from a metal sheet. For the same, how many m2 of the sheet are required? [Use ╧А = 22/7]
questions.explanation: Given: Height of cylinder (h) = 1 m Base diameter = 140 cm Formula Used: Radius (r) = Diameter2\frac{Diameter}{2}2DiameterтАЛтАЛтАЛ Total Surface Area of closed cylinder = 2╧Аr(h + r) Solution: Convert diameter to meters: 140 cm = 1.4 m Radius (r) = 1.42\frac{1.4}{2}21.4тАЛтАЛ = 0.7 m Total Surface Area = 2 ├Ч 227├Ч0.7├Ч(1+0.7)\frac{22}{7} ├Ч 0.7 ├Ч (1 + 0.7)722тАЛ├Ч0.7├Ч(1+0.7)тАЛтАЛ Area = 2 ├Ч 22 ├Ч 0.1 ├Ч 1.7 Area = 4.4 ├Ч 1.7 Area = 7.48 m2 Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are in the ratio of 1/9:1/11:1/13 and its perimeter is 718 cm . Find the length of its longest side in metre.
questions.explanation: Given: Ratio of sides = 1/9 : 1/11 : 1/13 Perimeter = 718 cm Formula Used: Perimeter = Sum of all sides Solution: Find a common base for the ratio by multiplying with the LCM of 9, 11, and 13 (which is 1287): Ratio = 12879:128711:128713\frac{1287}{9} : \frac{1287}{11} : \frac{1287}{13}91287тАЛ:111287тАЛ:131287тАЛтАЛтАЛ Ratio = 143 : 117 : 99 Let the sides be 143x, 117x, and 99x. Sum of the sides (Perimeter) = 143x + 117x + 99x = 359x We know the perimeter is 718 cm: 359x = 718 x = 2 The longest side corresponds to the largest ratio value (143): Longest side = 143 ├Ч 2 = 286 cm Convert the length to meters: 286 cm = 2.86 m Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: If (xтАУ3) is the HCF of x┬▓ тАУ 5x + 6 and x┬▓ тАУ kx + 9, then what is the value of k?
questions.explanation: Given Polynomial 1 = x2тИТ5x+6x^{2} - 5x + 6x2тИТ5x+6тАЛтАЛ Polynomial 2 = x2тИТkx+9x^{2} - kx + 9x2тИТkx+9тАЛтАЛ HCF of the polynomials = (x - 3) Formula Used Factor Theorem: If (x - a) is a factor of polynomial P(x), then P(a) = 0. Solution Since (x - 3) is the HCF, it must be a factor of x2тИТkx+9.x^{2} - kx + 9.x2тИТkx+9.тАЛтАЛ Substitute x = 3 into the second polynomial and equate to zero. тАЛ(3)2тИТk(3)+9=09тИТ3k+9=018тИТ3k=03k=18k=6(3)^{2} - k(3) + 9 = 0 \\9 - 3k + 9 = 0\\18 - 3k = 0\\3k = 18\\k = 6(3)2тИТk(3)+9=09тИТ3k+9=018тИТ3k=03k=18k=6тАЛтАЛ Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A number when divided by a divisor leaves a remainder of 25. When twice the original number is divided by the same divisor, the remainder is 13. What is the value of the divisor?
questions.explanation: Given: Remainder when a number is divided by a divisor = 25 Remainder when twice the number is divided by the same divisor = 13 Formula Used: Dividend = Divisor ├Ч Quotient + Remainder Divisor = 2├ЧR1тИТR2 2 ├Ч R_1 - R_22├ЧR1тАЛтИТR2тАЛтАЛтАЛ Solution: Let the number be N, divisor be D, and quotient be q. N = D ├Ч q + 25 When the number is doubled: 2N = 2(D ├Ч q + 25) = 2Dq + 50 When 2N is divided by D, the remainder will be the remainder when 50 is divided by D. Given remainder is 13. So, 50 = D ├Ч (some quotient) + 13 Since the remainder is 13, D must be greater than 13. 50 - 13 = D ├Ч (some quotient) 37 = D ├Ч (some quotient) Since 37 is a prime number, D must be 37. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b) тАЛ Exam Hall Method:
